pages.title: Get a GitHub Enterprise Cloud Pages site

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

pages.descriptionHTML:
Gets information about a GitHub Enterprise Cloud Pages site.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

schema.title: GitHub Pages
schema.description: The configuration for GitHub Pages for a repository.

url.description: The API address for accessing this Page resource.

status.description: The status of the most recent build of the Page.

protected_domain_state.description: The state if the domain is verified

pending_domain_unverified_at.description: The timestamp when a pending domain becomes unverified.

custom_404.description: Whether the Page has a custom 404 page.

html_url.description: The web address the Page can be accessed from.

build_type.description: The process in which the Page will be built.

public.description: Whether the GitHub Pages site is publicly visible. If set to `true`, the site is accessible to anyone on the internet. If set to `false`, the site will only be accessible to users who have at least `read` access to the repository that published the site.

domains.description: Array of the domain set and its alternate name (if it is configured)

https_enforced.description: Whether https is enabled on the domain

pages.title: Create a GitHub Enterprise Cloud Pages site

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

bodyParameters.name: build_type
bodyParameters.description: The process in which the Page will be built. Possible values are "legacy" and "workflow".

bodyParameters.name: source
bodyParameters.description: The source branch and directory used to publish your Pages site.

childParamsGroups.name: branch
childParamsGroups.description: The repository branch used to publish your site's source files.

childParamsGroups.name: path
childParamsGroups.description: The repository directory that includes the source files for the Pages site. Allowed paths are / or /docs. Default: /

pages.descriptionHTML:
Configures a GitHub Enterprise Cloud Pages site. For more information, see "About GitHub Pages."

The authenticated user must be a repository administrator, maintainer, or have the 'manage GitHub Pages settings' permission.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

schema.title: GitHub Pages
schema.description: The configuration for GitHub Pages for a repository.

url.description: The API address for accessing this Page resource.

status.description: The status of the most recent build of the Page.

protected_domain_state.description: The state if the domain is verified

pending_domain_unverified_at.description: The timestamp when a pending domain becomes unverified.

custom_404.description: Whether the Page has a custom 404 page.

html_url.description: The web address the Page can be accessed from.

build_type.description: The process in which the Page will be built.

public.description: Whether the GitHub Pages site is publicly visible. If set to `true`, the site is accessible to anyone on the internet. If set to `false`, the site will only be accessible to users who have at least `read` access to the repository that published the site.

domains.description: Array of the domain set and its alternate name (if it is configured)

https_enforced.description: Whether https is enabled on the domain

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

pages.title: Update information about a GitHub Enterprise Cloud Pages site

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

bodyParameters.name: cname
bodyParameters.description: Specify a custom domain for the repository. Sending a null value will remove the custom domain. For more about custom domains, see "Using a custom domain with GitHub Pages."

bodyParameters.name: https_enforced
bodyParameters.description: Specify whether HTTPS should be enforced for the repository.

bodyParameters.name: build_type
bodyParameters.description: The process by which the GitHub Pages site will be built. workflow means that the site is built by a custom GitHub Actions workflow. legacy means that the site is built by GitHub when changes are pushed to a specific branch.

bodyParameters.name: source
bodyParameters.description: Update the source for the repository. Must include the branch name and path.

childParamsGroups.name: branch
childParamsGroups.description: The repository branch used to publish your site's source files.

childParamsGroups.name: path
childParamsGroups.description: The repository directory that includes the source files for the Pages site. Allowed paths are / or /docs.

bodyParameters.name: public
bodyParameters.description: Configures access controls for the GitHub Pages site. If public is set to true, the site is accessible to anyone on the internet. If set to false, the site will only be accessible to users who have at least read access to the repository that published the site. This includes anyone in your Enterprise if the repository is set to internal visibility.

pages.descriptionHTML:
Updates information for a GitHub Enterprise Cloud Pages site. For more information, see "About GitHub Pages.

The authenticated user must be a repository administrator, maintainer, or have the 'manage GitHub Pages settings' permission.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

pages.title: List GitHub Enterprise Cloud Pages builds

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

pages.descriptionHTML:
Lists builts of a GitHub Enterprise Cloud Pages site.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

pages.title: Request a GitHub Enterprise Cloud Pages build

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

pages.descriptionHTML:
You can request that your site be built from the latest revision on the default branch. This has the same effect as pushing a commit to your default branch, but does not require an additional commit. Manually triggering page builds can be helpful when diagnosing build warnings and failures.

Build requests are limited to one concurrent build per repository and one concurrent build per requester. If you request a build while another is still in progress, the second request will be queued until the first completes.

parameters.name: owner

pages.title: Create a GitHub Pages deployment

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

bodyParameters.name: artifact_id
bodyParameters.description: The ID of an artifact that contains the .zip or .tar of static assets to deploy. The artifact belongs to the repository. Either artifact_id or artifact_url are required.

bodyParameters.name: artifact_url
bodyParameters.description: The URL of an artifact that contains the .zip or .tar of static assets to deploy. The artifact belongs to the repository. Either artifact_id or artifact_url are required.

bodyParameters.name: environment
bodyParameters.description: The target environment for this GitHub Pages deployment.

bodyParameters.name: pages_build_version
bodyParameters.description: A unique string that represents the version of the build for this deployment.

bodyParameters.name: oidc_token
bodyParameters.description: The OIDC token issued by GitHub Actions certifying the origin of the deployment.

pages.descriptionHTML:
Create a GitHub Pages deployment for a repository.

The authenticated user must have write permission to the repository.

id.description: The ID of the GitHub Pages deployment. This is the Git SHA of the deployed commit.

status_url.description: The URI to monitor GitHub Pages deployment status.

page_url.description: The URI to the deployed GitHub Pages.

preview_url.description: The URI to the deployed GitHub Pages preview.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

pages.title: Get the status of a GitHub Pages deployment

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: pages_deployment_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Pages deployment. You can also give the commit SHA of the deployment.

pages.descriptionHTML:
Gets the current status of a GitHub Pages deployment.

The authenticated user must have read permission for the GitHub Pages site.

status.description: The current status of the deployment.

pages.title: Cancel a GitHub Pages deployment

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: pages_deployment_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Pages deployment. You can also give the commit SHA of the deployment.

pages.descriptionHTML:
Cancels a GitHub Pages deployment.

The authenticated user must have write permissions for the GitHub Pages site.

response.description: A header with no content is returned.

statusCodes.description: A header with no content is returned.

pages.title: Get a DNS health check for GitHub Pages

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

pages.descriptionHTML:
Gets a health check of the DNS settings for the CNAME record configured for a repository's GitHub Pages.

The first request to this endpoint returns a 202 Accepted status and starts an asynchronous background task to get the results for the domain. After the background task completes, subsequent requests to this endpoint return a 200 OK status with the health check results in the response.

The authenticated user must be a repository administrator, maintainer, or have the 'manage GitHub Pages settings' permission to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

statusCodes.description: Custom domains are not available for GitHub Pages
There isn't a CNAME for this page
